result.stdout:
Description:
  A .NET tool for working with OpenShift

Usage:
  dotnet-shift [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  login               Log in to a server
  deploy <PROJECT>    Deploy a .NET application to OpenShift [default: .]
  list                List deployments
  delete <COMPONENT>  Delete a component
  context             Operate on connection contexts

commands.payload:
Description:
  A .NET tool for working with OpenShift

Usage:
  dotnet-shift [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  login               Log in to a server
  deploy <PROJECT>    Deploy a .NET application to OpenShift [default: .]
  list                List deployments
  delete <COMPONENT>  Delete a component
  context             Operate on connection contexts

result.stdout:
Description:
  Operate on connection contexts

Usage:
  dotnet-shift context [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  get               Print information about the current context
  list              List connection contexts
  set <CONTEXT>     Set the current connection context
  delete <CONTEXT>  Delete the specified connection context

commands.payload:

  list              List connection contexts
  set <CONTEXT>     Set the current connection context
  delete <CONTEXT>  Delete the specified connection context

Usage:

Arguments:
  <CONTEXT>

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Delete a component

Usage:
  dotnet-shift delete <COMPONENT> [options]

Arguments:
  <COMPONENT>

Options:
  --context <CONTEXT>  Connection context [default: current context]
  -f, --force          Ignore conditions that warrant aborting the operation
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Deploy a .NET application to OpenShift

Usage:
  dotnet-shift deploy [<PROJECT>] [options]

Arguments:
  <PROJECT>  [default: .]

Options:
  --context <CONTEXT>  Connection context [default: current context]
  --expose             Make the application accessible externally
  --name <COMPONENT>   Name the component
  --no-build           Do not start a build
  --no-follow          Do not follow progress
  --part-of <APP>      Add to application
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Deploy a .NET application to OpenShift

Usage:
  dotnet-shift deploy [<PROJECT>] [options]

Arguments:
  <PROJECT>  [default: .]

Options:
  --context <CONTEXT>  Connection context [default: current context]
  --expose             Make the application accessible externally
  --name <COMPONENT>   Name the component
  --no-build           Do not start a build
  --no-follow          Do not follow progress
  --part-of <APP>      Add to application
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Log in to a server

Usage:
  dotnet-shift login [options]

Options:
  --insecure-skip-tls-verify  If true, the server's certificate will not be checked for validity (insecure)
  --name <CONTEXT>            Name the connection
  --namespace <NAMESPACE>     Kubernetes namespace
  --server (REQUIRED)         Address and port of the Kubernetes API server
  --token (REQUIRED)          Bearer token for authentication to the API server
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

Usage:

Options:

  --name <CONTEXT>            Name the connection
  --namespace <NAMESPACE>     Kubernetes namespace
  --server (REQUIRED)         Address and port of the Kubernetes API server
  --token (REQUIRED)          Bearer token for authentication to the API server
  -?, -h, --help              Show help and usage information
